Extracting manuscript results from figures for Nature papoer

Nature manuscript — iterative extraction

This document embeds each figure and prints extracted numbers only (no code in the body).
Live in Scripts/Nature_Revision2/extracting_ms_results/; you can knit from BuildFinalFigures, from Scripts/Nature_Revision2, or from this folder. Set NR2_PROJECT_ROOT to the repo root if needed.

Forest plot (P = 0.5 vs reference, all-primary start)

Open PDF (relative to this report)

Extracted values (same data as the figure)

One row per scenario; each outcome is a column. Scenario letters A–E match the forest x-axis. Megatrees and carbon include 80% intervals on the change where the figure does; birds and beetles are point estimates only.

All-primary start: change from reference (P = 0) to P = 0.5. Bracketed values are 80% intervals on the change (megatrees and carbon only).
Scenario Plantation Old-growth birds Old-growth beetles Megatrees (% landscape) Cumulative C (billion MgC)
C Without plantation -0.118 -0.137 -3.782 [-3.981, -3.616] -2.499 [-3.044, -1.938]
D Without plantation -0.156 -0.136 -6.752 [-6.888, -6.624] -1.403 [-1.717, -1.094]
E Without plantation -0.148 -0.112 -5.469 [-5.628, -5.342] -2.158 [-2.596, -1.732]
A With plantation -0.044 -0.022 -0.963 [-1.195, -0.744] -0.384 [-0.416, -0.345]
B With plantation -0.048 -0.024 -1.053 [-1.284, -0.836] -0.442 [-0.480, -0.398]

To refresh the forest PDF: Rscript Scripts/Nature_Revision2/03_forest_allprimary_p05_vs_p0.R.

Environmental modified figure — primary start (P ≈ 0.1 vs P = 1)

This matches the AllPrimary column of environmental_by_starting_3landscapes.pdf (modified geom style from 02_build_modified_final_figures.R, run ACTIVE_RUN=environmental_by_starting).

Open PDF (relative to this report)

Extracted outcomes — min / max / range by starting landscape

How to read the tables. Each block is one starting landscape. Under P ≈ 0.1 and P = 1.0 you get: composition of the global min-mean portfolio, its mean (and [lo, hi] for megatrees/carbon), then the same for the max-mean portfolio. The Δ row is the change in (max − min) from the low- to the high-P band, with a parenthetical (lower / upper) pair when model bounds allow. The last row is median national timber (Mm³) in those bands.

CY_D deforestation-on scenarios only. Birds/beetles: loser group; megatrees: 50 m threshold; carbon: cumulative stock difference vs baseline (60 yr, slope 1), billion MgC. Within each starting landscape, production_target is restricted to ±0.03 of 0.1 and of 1.0, with one grid point per portfolio (closest to band centre). Min / max scenarios are global argmin / argmax by mean outcome in that band. Composition: OG / pl / 1L / 2L as % of national landscape at the snapped grid for those portfolios. Min / Max scenario rows show the mean and, for megatrees (95%) and carbon (80%), mean [lo, hi] on that portfolio; birds/beetles show the mean only. After each production-target block, Range (max − min) is max(mean)−min(mean) in that band; meg/carbon append (lower / upper) on that range when model bounds exist. Rows under Between production targets compare across P≈0.1 vs P=1 (Δ in range; median timber). Median timber uses the same scenario file and ±0.03 bands in MasterAllScenarios.

To rebuild the environmental PDF, set ACTIVE_RUN=environmental_by_starting and run Scripts/Nature_Revision2/02_build_modified_final_figures.R from this project root.
